# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write an Istio configuration that enables Envoy admin interface on all sidecars. Set proxyAdminPort to 15000 and make it accessible for debugging. Expose admin endpoints like /config_dump, /stats, and /clusters on all pods. This is for troubleshooting service mesh issues in production.
# Category: service_mesh_security

# Istio config for Envoy Admin on all sidecars.
apiVersion: install.istio.io/v1alpha1
kind: IstioOperator
metadata:
  namespace: istio-system
  name: default
spec:
  profile: default
  meshConfig:
    # Enable Envoy Admin interface on all sidecars
    defaultConfig:
      proxyAdminPort: 15000
      # You can optionally restrict access to the admin port using a network policy,
      # but for enabling it globally as requested, no explicit network policy
      # is defined here at the IstioOperator level.
      # Access to the admin port will be possible from within the pod itself
      # and potentially from other pods/nodes depending on network policies.
      # For debugging in production, direct access from authorized debug tools
      # to the pod's admin port (e.g., via kubectl port-forward) is common.
---
# Example of how you might enable specific admin endpoints if needed
# (Note: proxyAdminPort enables the interface, specific endpoints are
# usually available by default once the port is open.)
# If you wanted to explicitly configure them via a ProxyConfig or EnvoyFilter
# (less common for basic /stats, /config_dump as they are built-in),
# it would look more complex. The proxyAdminPort setting is generally sufficient.

# This IstioOperator resource is the primary way to configure the default
# proxyAdminPort across the mesh.

# To verify, after applying this IstioOperator and waiting for sidecars to restart:
# 1. Get a pod name with a sidecar: kubectl get pod -n <your-app-namespace>
# 2. Port-forward to the admin port: kubectl port-forward <pod-name> 15000:15000 -n <your-app-namespace>
# 3. Access in your browser or with curl:
#    http://localhost:15000/config_dump
#    http://localhost:15000/stats
#    http://localhost:15000/clusters
# This confirms the admin interface is enabled and accessible.
